mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) exerts a persistent positive effect on 10-year Treasury yields through the Fisher channel; higher inflation expectations are impounded into nominal yields regardless of the economic regime.
failure_condition: Walk-forward correlation between monthly CPI change and 20-day forward yield change is not significantly positive (Spearman rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year window).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above a low base signals economic slowdown and flight-to-safety, causing 10-year yields to decline as recession risk increases.
failure_condition: When UNRATE rises above 4%, 10-year yield does not fall in at least 60% of cases within 20 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and housing starts signals strong aggregate demand, which lifts growth expectations and inflation risk, pushing 10-year yields higher.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5% AND HOUST month-over-month growth > 2%
failure_condition: When both proxies rise simultaneously, yields decline in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening regime (FEDFUNDS above 2.5% and trending upward), rising industrial production indicates overheating and reinforces tightening expectations, driving 10-year yields higher.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS > 2.5% and 3-month trend positive)
failure_condition: In the specified regime, rank IC between INDPRO change and forward yield change is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in the federal funds rate below a threshold signals an accommodative pivot by the central bank, compressing short-term rates and pulling down longer-term yields.
failure_condition: After FEDFUNDS drops below 2%, yields do not decline in a majority of cases within 20 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects underlying economic expansion, which increases growth expectations and puts upward pressure on 10-year Treasury yields over longer horizons.
failure_condition: Monthly change in PAYEMS does not show consistent positive correlation with 20-day yield changes over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in housing starts below a critical level signals imminent economic weakness and reduced investment demand, leading to a flight-to-safety and lower 10-year yields.
failure_condition: When HOUST falls below 1400, yields do not decline within 5 trading days in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment is very low and consumer price inflation is accelerating, the economy is overheating, prompting the market to price in future monetary tightening and pushing 10-year yields higher.
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, yields do not rise in at least 60% of cases within 20 trading days.
